title: read_image_file returns 0-byte base64 on empty file as success (perimeter defense)
description:
## Severity
Trivial

## Dimension
Perimeter Defense / Functional Correctness

## Location
src/tools/file/read.rs:40-56 (read_image_file)

## Violation
On a 0-byte image file, the function returns a successful result with a base64-empty payload (test read_image_empty_file_returns_ok_with_empty_base64 pins this as a CC parity gap). The downstream Anthropic vision API will reject the empty base64 with an opaque 400 — but only after the request leaves the client and consumes a turn. The classic perimeter-defense violation: fail fast at the boundary, not after the API call.

Also, the function always allocates a String the size of (1.33 × file bytes) for the base64 output even when the file is huge. There is no cap on file size before encoding.

## Mandated refactor
1. Reject 0-byte image files at this boundary with an error message: "Image file '{path}' is empty (0 bytes)."
2. Add an upper cap (e.g. 10 MB) — anything larger should error or be downscaled, not base64-bloated by 33% into the prompt.
3. Probe the magic bytes (`infer` or `image::guess_format`) to verify the file actually is the claimed mime type — currently `mime_type` is decided purely by extension in detect_file_type, so a renamed binary will be encoded as image/png.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
